items.title: [正面] went in with low expectations (gacha game after al...
items.summary:
went in with low expectations (gacha game after all) but got surprised

Positives:
- Lovecraftian-ish horror story is pretty decent (at least how far i'v gotten to, characters also feel fleshed out and not flat as its usually the case in mobiletype games)
- Spirelike battle system has good variety (4 main build types, with att/defensive sub-builds depending what characters you bring) tho in the beginning will need to stick to one, cuz powering up toons ain't extremely fast
- art is adequate and juicy, same as skill effects

Some cons
- some translation errors here and there (especially in skill descriptions, but with some brain power overcomable)
 - energy system (wouldn't be a gatcha game if it hadn't, but at least they allow auto-completing finished grind stages, so not so greedy with time sinks)

All in all, a slay the spire waifu (some husbandu too) with a mystical worlds bad-happenings on top of it game, where you need to wait for next story beats day or two. If that isn't turn off, i recommend checking out.

items.title: [正面] So far so good, great art and atmosphere, good mus...
items.summary:
So far so good, great art and atmosphere, good music. Gacha elements are extremely generous, you can literally just select characters with some of the introduction items and start with a team.  Several teams, actually, since it's surprising how well one character can fit into multiple teams. With just pulling 1 character from the standard banner and picking up the 4 freebies along with the 4 basic units you receive as starters (and those are actually good, too), I could make a poison team, a strength-stack/multistrike team, and a team entirely made of smug-looking girls. And the smug girls still have the bases covered. This is important because the game isn't messing around and expects you to grasp the basic concepts asap. To help with this, there is a tutorial, a glossary, and a little challenge tutorial where you can take individual characters into a solo battle to learn their mechanics, even if you don't own that character.

I just like smug-looking anime girls, alright?
